refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency is within 150 ms p95 to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies a high-level latency requirement for teleoperation safety. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) clarifies the performance target and safety relevance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end communication latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation scenarios to verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication transmission delay over the network link.
FR2: Optimize processing and response time within teleoperation control systems.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator with minimal buffering.
FR4: Ensure reliable network link availability and error handling to prevent latency spikes.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use low-latency communication protocols and network prioritization.
DP2: Implement edge computing for local processing to reduce round-trip delays.
DP3: Design efficient telemetry data compression and prioritization algorithms.
DP4: Integrate network quality monitoring and adaptive control to maintain latency.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use low-latency communication protocols and network prioritization.

potential_sources: Interaction between telemetry data compression (DP3) and communication protocol (DP1)
Dependency on network link quality affecting latency (DP4)
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on communication protocol optimization, which is logically separable from processing and telemetry functions, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unpredictable network congestion.
Assumes teleoperation control system can handle prioritized traffic without bottlenecks.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating adaptive network quality monitoring (DP4) in subsequent refinement to mitigate latency risks.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realizes communication protocol optimization and prioritization for teleoperation latency control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation control and telemetry data over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time telemetry data to the operator and other system modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as communication protocol optimization is core to Teleoperation module; low diffusion risk.
Broader system scope but less focused containment for latency-specific DP.
Vehicle module less relevant for communication protocol; higher diffusion risk if assigned here.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not implement communication latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes latency control, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry if not well defined.
Dependency on network infrastructure outside module control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Network Link and Telemetry modules to reduce integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear performance focus and measurable criteria.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is well contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as best ownership candidate, reducing cross-module diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry latency measurements is feasible with current instrumentation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Teleoperation and Network Link modules.
Plan for adaptive network quality monitoring in next decomposition level.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and integration of adaptive network monitoring DP is needed to address latency risks comprehensively.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication transmission delay over the network link.
FR2: Optimize processing and response time within teleoperation control systems.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator with minimal buffering.
FR4: Ensure reliable network link availability and error handling to prevent latency spikes.

revise.why: To incorporate adaptive network quality monitoring and processing optimizations to mitigate latency risks.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement prioritized communication protocols.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network congestion.
Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Network Link.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational network conditions.
Interface performance benchmarks between Teleoperation and Network Link.
